t_ch: 呜呜呜…你太会说了。
t_en: Waaa... You're too good with words.

t_ch: （表达积极的情绪）
t_en: (Express positivity)

t_ch: 请粉丝们放心，卢卡选手正在积极训练努力恢复，我们教练组也在总结复盘。我们会抓住复活赛的机会，各位等待我们王者归来吧！
t_en: The fans needn't worry. Luka is already training hard to get back into form, and our coaching team is reflecting on our past missteps. We will treasure the chance in the repechage, so everyone, please wait patiently for the return of our champion!

t_ch: 可是我看卢卡还挺消沉的……
t_en: But I've noticed Luka is still pretty down in the dumps...

t_ch: 请这位记者朋友注意分辨信息来源。
t_en: I would remind this journalist to be discerning about your information sources.

t_ch: （质疑卢卡的影响力）
t_en: (Question Luka's popularity)

t_ch: 真的假的？卢卡有这么多的粉丝？
t_en: Really? Luka has this many fans?

t_ch: 啊，这个问题我还真没准备…那就，祝粉丝朋友们身体健康。
t_en: Ah, I didn't prepare for this question... Then, we hope for our fans' good health and prosperity.

t_ch: 喂！你到底会不会开发布会啊！
t_en: Hey! Do you know how to conduct a press conference or not!

t_ch: 好了，这次提问就到这里了。
t_en: All right. That's it for your questions.

t_ch: 前排左手边第三位。
t_en: Front row, third from the left.

t_ch: 请这位记者朋友提问。
t_en: Please ask your questions.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}教练你好，我是来自《武林风云》的记者。我们是一档专业武术点评栏目，我的名字不重要，您可以叫我「专业记者」。
t_en: Hello, Coach {NICKNAME}. I'm from the Martial Herald. We are a professional martial arts commentary column. My name isn't important, and you can just call me "Professional Journalist."

t_ch: 比起卢卡选手本人的临场发挥，我更关心教练您的战术安排。
t_en: Luka's performance in the ring aside, I'm more concerned about your tactical measures.

t_ch: 你请问。
t_en: Ask away.

t_ch: 涉及机密部分我会拒绝回答。
t_en: I'll have to refrain from revealing any classified information.

t_ch: 放心，我是专业的，规矩我都懂。
t_en: Don't worry. I'm a professional, and I know the rules.

t_ch: 等的就是你这样的专业记者。
t_en: I've been waiting for a professional individual such as yourself.

t_ch: 我反复观看了比赛回放，有一个惊人发现——卢卡选手在面对枪口时，并没有采用常规的快速躲闪方式应对，而是一动不动地站在原地。
t_en: I watched the match replay back to back multiple times, and made an astounding discovery — when Luka was facing the gun chamber, he didn't employ any common tactics to quickly dodge away, but stood rooted on the spot.

t_ch: 很多人误以为这是卢卡选手的失误，但是我相信这是您布置的战术。以不动的方式应对对手的敏捷，让波提欧选手产生疑惑，获得心理博弈上的优势！
t_en: Many mistook this for Luka's error, but I'm willing to believe this was a tactic that you planned. Deploying a method of immobility against the opponent's agility, to sow the seed of doubt in Boothill, and to gain the upper hand in the psychological battle!

t_ch: 对对对！我就是这么想的…
t_en: Yes, yes, absolutely! That was my plan exactly...

t_ch: 什么叫专业？这个就叫专业！和我安排的战术一模一样。
t_en: What is professionalism? This is professionalism! As professional as my planned tactic.

t_ch: 唉…可惜卢卡没沉住气，浪费了绝好的机会。
t_en: But... it was a pity Luka couldn't stand his ground at the end and squandered this golden opportunity.

t_ch: 我的猜测果然没错！
t_en: I knew I was right on the money!

t_ch: 我怎么没想到你这个战术…
t_en: Why didn't I think of that...?

t_ch: 说实话，我没有想到这样的战术。也许你应该加入我们的教练组……
t_en: Truth be told, I hadn't thought of such a thing. Maybe you should join our coaching team...

t_ch: 哈？{NICKNAME}教练，这个时候真的就不要演了吧。
t_en: Eh? Coach {NICKNAME}, this isn't the time to play dumb.

t_ch: 我是…真的没想到。
t_en: I... really didn't think of that.

t_ch: 我明白了，比赛还没有彻底结束，还不能透露你的战术目的。
t_en: I get it. You can't reveal your tactical objectives before the tournament truly ends.

t_ch: 你脑补的也太多了…
t_en: You sure are agile with your mental gymnastics...

t_ch: 你脑补的也太多了，其实我也没想明白卢卡为什么不躲一躲。
t_en: You're overthinking it. I've never really understood why Luka didn't evade.

t_ch: 不过躲也没用吧，毕竟那是枪……
t_en: Though there probably wasn't much point in dodging. I mean, it was a gun after all...

t_ch: 你是在质疑我的专业能力吗？
t_en: Are you doubting my professionalism?

t_ch: 我是专业的，我明白教练此刻还在隐藏战术意图，具体的我就不问了。
t_en: I'm a professional, and I understand that you wish to keep your tactics under wraps. I won't delve into details then.

t_ch: 感谢{NICKNAME}教练解答我的疑惑。
t_en: Thank you, Coach {NICKNAME}, for resolving my uncertainties.

t_ch: 其实卢卡选手想要战胜波提欧也并非完全不可能，我有一计，请教练细品。
t_en: Actually, it's not impossible that Luka could defeat Boothill. I have a plan, so please bear with me.

t_ch: 所谓「七步以外枪快，七步以内拳快」，若是一开局卢卡选手能快速缩近双方距离，然后贴脸出拳，请问波提欧选手又该如何应对？
t_en: As the saying goes: The gun is faster outside of seven strides, and the fist is quicker within seven. If Luka could rapidly close the distance once the match started, then strike a punch at point-blank range, how would Boothill respond to that?

t_ch: 七步之内拳也不快…
t_en: The fist isn't all that fast within seven strides, either...

t_ch: 七步之外枪快，七步之内枪又准又快。
t_en: The gun may be quicker outside of seven strides, but it's also quick AND precise within seven...

t_ch: 我建议你少看幻戏，多看实战。
t_en: I'd suggest you lay off the immersia and pay attention to actual fights.

t_ch: 你是在质疑我的专业能力吗？
t_en: Are you doubting my professionalism?

t_ch: 那么要怎么缩进距离呢…
t_en: How would you close the distance then...

t_ch: 那么，开局之后卢卡选手要如何快速缩近双方的距离呢？
t_en: Then, how would Luka be able to close the distance quickly after the match starts?

t_ch: 只要全力奔跑一定可以做到的。
t_en: As long as he sprints forth with all his being, he'll surely be able to make it.

t_ch: 几乎是所有人都被专业记者的想法震住了。
t_en: It appears that everyone in the room is dumbfounded by the professional journalist's plan.

t_ch: 怎么，你们是在质疑我的专业能力吗？
t_en: What, you all doubting my professionalism?

t_ch: 下场你来打波提欧…
t_en: You fight Boothill in the next match then...

t_ch: 我很看好你的战术，下场你来打波提欧。
t_en: I've got my money on your strat. You go fight Boothill in the next match.

t_ch: 我打波提欧？真的假的！
t_en: Me? Fight Boothill? You've gotta be kidding!

t_ch: 好了，这次提问就到这里了。
t_en: All right. That's it for your questions.

t_ch: 现在请{NICKNAME}教练再选一个记者提问。
t_en: Coach {NICKNAME}, please choose another journalist.

t_ch: 前排左手边第一位。
t_en: Front row, first from the left.

t_ch: 前排左手边第二位。
t_en: Front row, second from the left.

t_ch: 前排左手边第三位。
t_en: Front row, third from the left.

t_ch: 感谢{NICKNAME}教练的精彩答复，今天的采访环节到此结束，请各位来场的媒体朋友有序离场。
t_en: Thank you, Coach {NICKNAME}, for your insightful answers. Today's Q&A ends here. Media personnel, please leave the area in an orderly and civil fashion.

t_ch: 例行的采访环节已经结束，接下来该去办正事了。
t_en: Now that the routine interview is over, it's time to get down to business.

t_ch: 首先恭喜卢卡选手获得复活赛胜利，关于这场比赛，我们再次邀请到了{NICKNAME}教练接受记者提问。
t_en: First off, congrats to Luka for winning the repechage. We have once again invited Coach {NICKNAME} to answer journalist questions about this match.

t_ch: 到场的各位记者朋友请在会场中找到合适位置，保持距离，并请按照流程准备好提问。
t_en: To the journalists who have arrived at the site, please situate yourselves appropriately, maintain your distance, and get your questions ready according to procedure.

t_ch: 相信记者朋友们一定准备了很多问题，那我们抓紧时间让{NICKNAME}教练选择记者提问，请注意控制发言时长，现在开始。
t_en: I trust you all must have prepared many questions. Let's not waste time and have Coach {NICKNAME} pick the journalist. Please pay attention to time. We're starting now.

t_ch: 前排左手边第一位。
t_en: Front row, first from the left.

t_ch: 请这位记者朋友提问。
t_en: Please ask your questions.

t_ch: 我是来自星际和平娱乐的实习记者卡美丽，{NICKNAME}教练，首先恭喜你和卢卡选手打赢复活赛。
t_en: I'm Carmella from Interastral Peace Entertainment. Coach {NICKNAME}, firstly, congratulations on your and Luka's repechage win.

t_ch: 众所周知，银枝先生身为纯美骑士，对挑战复活赛的选手考核十分严格，听说他不仅要求选手具备强大的实力，还需要展现足够的「纯美」。
t_en: It's common knowledge that as a Knight of Beauty, Mr. Argenti's evaluation of those in the repechage is highly stringent. I heard he not only requests that his opponents possess great strength, but they also have to exhibit sufficient Beauty.

t_ch: 关于卢卡选手的纯美，{NICKNAME}教练是如何理解的？
t_en: What is Coach {NICKNAME}'s understanding of Luka's Beauty?

t_ch: 他长得那么帅就挺美的…
t_en: He's pretty beautiful with how handsome he is...

t_ch: 请允许我用一些*比较通俗的标准*来看待这个问题——仅从外貌体格上来看，卢卡选手毫无疑问是一名帅哥，他已经足够「纯美」了。
t_en: Let me put it in "more crass" terms: just based on looks and physique, Luka is undeniably handsome and already very "Beautiful."

t_ch: 即便是见多识广的纯美骑士银枝，也应该承认卢卡选手通过「纯美」的考核绰绰有余。
t_en: Even the immensely knowledgeable Knight of Beauty, Argenti, would admit that Luka passes the evaluation of Beauty.

t_ch: 心地善良的就是纯美的…
t_en: Compassion and kindness are a kind of beauty in itself...

t_ch: 要我说，「纯美」首先应该是心灵美。我知道卢卡曾经与一位小女孩玛吉之间的故事，从那件事看出，卢卡实在是个心地善良的家伙。
t_en: I think that compassion and kindness should be the foremost benchmark of Beauty. I know of the story between Luka and Margie, a little girl he once met. Just off that incident alone, Luka is indeed a compassionate fellow.

t_ch: 就算是颇为苛刻的纯美骑士，肯定也会承认卢卡的纯美之心！
t_en: Even the most rigorous of the Knights of Beauty would recognize that Luka has a heart fitting for Beauty!

t_ch: 大方自信就是美…
t_en: Beauty lies in generosity and confidence...

t_ch: 我们没考虑那么多。但我觉得作为一个选手，没有什么是比大方自信的展现自己的技巧更美。
t_en: We've never thought about it much, but I think as a contestant, there's nothing more beautiful than showing your own skill in a generous and confident fashion.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}教练的理解果然很独特。
t_en: Coach {NICKNAME}'s understanding is certainly unique.

t_ch: 虽然卢卡选手成功返回赛场，但接下来的对手全是个顶个的强者，不知道{NICKNAME}教练对后面的比赛是否有信心获取胜利？
t_en: Though Contestant Luka has successfully returned to the tournament, the opponents lined up for him are all undisputed powerhouses. I wonder if Coach {NICKNAME} is confident of victory for the upcoming matches?

t_ch: 总之，干就完事了…
t_en: Just fight and don't think of anything else...

t_ch: 我们现在状态火热，信心十足，谁遇到我们谁倒霉。不管对手是人是鬼，全都一样，干就完事了！
t_en: We're raring to go right now, and we're pumped. Whoever is matched against us is out of luck. It's all the same — human or ghost, let's just fight them!

t_ch: 这可真是太有自信了！
t_en: What a super confident answer!

t_ch: 希望对手下手轻点…
t_en: I hope the opponent pulls their punches...

t_ch: 实话实说，我不是很有信心。毕竟前面的比赛已经打得很艰难了，后面的选手只会更加厉害，眼下只能寄希望于对手下手轻点吧。
t_en: Honestly, I'm not totally confident. The previous matches were already difficult, and the upcoming opponents will only get stronger. All I can hope for is that our opponents don't hit too hard.

t_ch: 看来比赛前景不容乐观啊，加油{NICKNAME}教练，加油卢卡！
t_en: Looks like you have a bleak outlook on the subsequent matches. Keep your heads up, Coach {NICKNAME} and Luka!

t_ch: 都这样了，随缘吧…
t_en: What's done is done. It's all up to luck now...

t_ch: 能走到这里已经超越我的预期了，接下来走一步看一步吧，享受比赛的过程更重要。
t_en: My expectations have already been exceeded by coming so far. It's just one foot ahead of the next right now. Enjoying the journey is much more important.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}教练的心态真好。
t_en: You really look on the bright side, Coach {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 好了，这次提问就到这里了。
t_en: All right. That's it for your questions.

t_ch: 前排左手边第二位。
t_en: Front row, second from the left.

t_ch: 请这位记者朋友提问。
t_en: Please ask your questions.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}教练，我是仙舟精武新闻网特邀记者桂乃芬。
t_en: Coach {NICKNAME}, I'm Guinaifen, special correspondent from the Xianzhou Martial Elite Network.

t_ch: 我为了宣传卢卡的复活赛，在直播里把嗓子喊哑了，{NICKNAME}教练您是不是应该表示一下？
t_en: To promote Luka's repechage, I screamed till I went hoarse during my live stream. Don't you have something to say about that, Coach {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 真是多谢了你啊。
t_en: I really must thank you.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，卢卡选手值得咱这么卖力。
t_en: Heh-heh, Luka is someone who's worth our efforts.

t_ch: 咱们俩这谁跟谁啊！
t_en: Hey, I thought we were buds!

t_ch: 哈哈，就是就是…喂！你就打算这么糊弄过去啊！
t_en: Haha, that's right... Hey! You're just gonna gloss over this?

t_ch: 这是在向我卖人情吗？
t_en: You trying to do me a favor or something?

t_ch: 喂喂，我们俩这么说就太生疏了吧。
t_en: Hey hey, it's not like we don't know each other.

t_ch: 其实我的第一个问题是关于「弃赛风波」的。外界传闻，卢卡选手输给波提欧之后，意志消沉，原本打算放弃参加复活赛的，幸好后来完成了比赛。
t_en: My first question is about the rumored match forfeiture. There's been a lot of buzz that Contestant Luka sank into a depressive state after losing to Boothill, and that he was prepared to forfeit the repechage, but fortunately still completed in the end.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}教练，这「弃赛风波」是真的么？
t_en: Coach {NICKNAME}, is the match forfeiting true?

t_ch: 确实有这回事…
t_en: That was indeed the case...

t_ch: 确实有这回事。幸亏卢卡及时从低谷中走了出来，现在想来有点后怕。
t_en: That was indeed the case, but luckily Luka managed to get himself out of it. Just the thought of it gives me the shivers.

t_ch: 每个人都会想要放弃的时候，有这样的想法是人之常情。但真正的英雄从来都是那些能正视自身弱小的人——毫无疑问，卢卡就是这样的人。
t_en: Everyone experiences moments when they feel like giving up. Such thoughts are only human. But real heroes are those who confront their own weaknesses — without a doubt, Luka is one of them.

t_ch: 不存在这回事…
t_en: There was no such thing...

t_ch: 不存在这回事，是大家多虑了。
t_en: There was no such thing. Everyone is just overthinking.

t_ch: 一点小小的挫折根本无法打倒卢卡选手，他可是熔锤出身的勇士，无敌的磐岩冠军！
t_en: A minor setback won't defeat Contestant Luka. He's a warrior forged by the hammer, the invincible Boulder Champion!

t_ch: 想弃赛的其实是我…
t_en: The one who wanted to forfeit was me...

t_ch: 其实意志消沉的是我，想弃赛的也是我。我好想永远待在没人的角落里。
t_en: Actually, I was the one who was feeling down, and it was also me who wanted to forfeit the match. I just want to stay in a corner where no one can ever find me.

t_ch: 不过请大家放心，卢卡选手非常积极，而我也已经从阴霾中走了出来……
t_en: But everyone can rest assured. Luka is very positive, and I've already stepped away from the gloom...

t_ch: 原来「弃赛风波」真相是这样的……
t_en: So this is the truth behind the match-forfeiting drama...

t_ch: 第二个问题——鉴于卢卡选手人气高涨，想必有许多品牌方都想邀请他合作。请问{NICKNAME}教练，方便透露些商业合作上的信息吗？
t_en: Second question — given Luka's soaring popularity, many brands must be eager to invite him for a collaboration. Coach {NICKNAME}, can you share some information regarding any commercial partnerships?

t_ch: 啊？从没考虑过…
t_en: Ah? I've never considered that...

t_ch: 感谢各个品牌方的支持。不过对于卢卡来说，当下最高优先级的目标仍是获得比赛胜利，除此之外的事情暂时没有考虑。
t_en: We are thankful for the support from all the brands. However, for Luka, his highest priority now is winning the match. For the time being, we will not be considering other matters.

t_ch: 如果品牌方们不嫌弃，希望在演武仪典赛程结束之后联系卢卡选手经纪人商谈合作的事项。
t_en: If any brands are interested, please contact Luka's agent after the Wardance to discuss any collaboration opportunities.

t_ch: 需要考察企业的实力…
t_en: We need to assess the companies' capacities...

t_ch: 确实有这方面考虑。不过我们团队十分重视卢卡选手的品牌价值，所以对于合作意象比较谨慎，需要考察企业的实力之后才会有进一步的消息。
t_en: We are definitely considering it. However, our team places great importance on Luka's brand value, so we are careful about collaboration opportunities. We will need to thoroughly assess the capabilities of the company before making any announcements.

t_ch: 真的有那么高的人气吗？
t_en: Is he really that popular?

t_ch: …接广告这种事，我好像不知道？真的假的，怎么没人给我说呢？
t_en: ...Taking on advertisements? I'm not in the know. Is this true? Why hasn't anyone told me?

t_ch: 回头我去问问。
t_en: I'll ask about it later.

t_ch: 好了，这次提问就到这里了。
t_en: All right. That's it for your questions.

t_ch: 前排左手边第三位。
t_en: Front row, third from the left.

t_ch: 请这位记者朋友提问。
t_en: Please ask your questions.

t_ch: 终于轮到我了！我是《周刊闻椿》的记者，请叫我「八卦记者」就行。
t_en: It's finally my turn! I'm a journalist from Weekly Wenchun. Please call me "Tabloid Journalist."

t_ch: 据说您与银枝先生交情匪浅，银枝先生甚至还暧昧地称呼你为「挚友」。
t_en: It's said that you and Mr. Argenti are close, and he also rather intimately refers to you as "dear friend."

t_ch: 请问{NICKNAME}教练，这层关系是否影响了银枝先生对卢卡选手的对决？
t_en: Coach {NICKNAME}, did this layer of relationship affect the match between Mr. Argenti and Contestant Luka?

t_ch: 我和银枝完全不熟。
t_en: I'm not familiar at all with Argenti.

t_ch: 你别张口就来，我和银枝完全不熟。
t_en: You should think before you open your mouth. I'm completely unfamiliar with Argenti.

t_ch: 真的假的？怎么感觉你很心虚。
t_en: Serious? Why do you look so guilty?

t_ch: 银枝不是那种人…
t_en: Argenti is not that type of person...

t_ch: 银枝作为纯美骑士团的骑士，不会做出有违骑士精神的事情。
t_en: As a member of the Knights of Beauty, Argenti will never violate the spirit of knighthood.

t_ch: 我看未必吧。
t_en: I don't think so.

t_ch: 你直接去问银枝啊…
t_en: You can go ask Argenti yourself...

t_ch: 你这么感兴趣，直接去问银枝啊。
t_en: If you're so interested, go ask Argenti yourself.

t_ch: 你待会别走，发布会结束我们俩就去！
t_en: Don't leave after this is over then. We'll go together right after the press conference ends!

t_ch: 请你立刻出去！
t_en: Please remove yourself from the premises immediately!

t_ch: 请你立刻出去！
t_en: Please remove yourself from the premises immediately!

t_ch: 急眼了急眼了！素材来了！
t_en: OMG OMG! The coach's mad! Here comes the material!

t_ch: 这位「八卦记者」请注意提问礼仪。
t_en: Tabloid Journalist, pay attention to your interview etiquette.

t_ch: 我还有一个问题！我看见十王司的工作人员进入过卢卡选手的休息室。我听说有些使用违禁兴奋剂的选手会遭到十王司调查……
t_en: I still have another question! I saw staff from the Ten-Lords Commission entering Contestant Luka's lounge. I heard that some contestants who were suspected of doping will be investigated by the Ten-Lords Commission...

t_ch: 关于这点，{NICKNAME}教练如何解释？
t_en: How will you explain this, Coach {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 就是朋友来探班…
t_en: It's just friends visiting...

t_ch: 你说的十王司工作人员是我朋友，来探探班而已。请你不要过度解读。
t_en: The Ten-Lords Commission staff that you speak of are my friends who were just visiting. Please do not over-analyze everything.

t_ch: 我真的是在过度解读吗？难道不是你在隐藏什么？
t_en: Am I really over-analyzing? Or are you hiding something?

t_ch: 他们是卢卡的粉丝…
t_en: They are Luka's fans...

t_ch: 咱们卢卡有路人缘，那个十王司的工作人员是他粉丝，要签名来的。
t_en: Our Luka has a good relationship with the public. The staff from the Ten-Lords Commission were fans and just wanted an autograph.

t_ch: 不过我还是得提醒下粉丝朋友们，要签名可以，不能打扰选手休息。
t_en: But I do want to remind our fans — It's okay to ask for an autograph, but please do not disturb the contestants during their rest.

t_ch: 要签名？随口编的理由我会信？难道不是你在隐藏什么？
t_en: Asking for an autograph? You really think I'll believe some lie you made up on the fly? Are you sure you're not hiding something?

t_ch: 你在侵犯选手的个人隐私…
t_en: You are infringing on the privacy of the contestant...

t_ch: 你这是在侵犯选手的个人隐私，我拒绝回答你！
t_en: You are infringing on the privacy of the contestant. I refuse to respond.

t_ch: 那就不要怪我乱猜了。
t_en: Then don't blame me for making my own guesses.

t_ch: 请你立刻出去！
t_en: Please remove yourself from the premises immediately!

t_ch: 请你立刻出去！
t_en: Please remove yourself from the premises immediately!

t_ch: 你看你，你好急。
t_en: Look at you, panicking.

t_ch: 你的提问次数用完了。
t_en: You've used up your allotted number of questions.

t_ch: 前排左手边第四位。
t_en: Front row, fourth from the left.

t_ch: 请这位记者朋友提问。
t_en: Please ask your questions.

t_ch: 欸？我怎么被点到了，我是不是站错位置了啊，其实我…连问题都没想好。
t_en: Eh? Why was I chosen? Am I standing in the wrong spot? Actually... I haven't even thought of the questions yet.

t_ch: 好尴尬呀…
t_en: How awkward...

t_ch: 你随便问点吧。
t_en: Just ask whatever.

t_ch: 请这位记者不要拖延时间，尽快提问。
t_en: Please refrain from wasting time and ask your questions quickly.

t_ch: …因为《曜青体育》的记者临时有事，我过来顶班，你叫我业余记者就好。
t_en: ...The journalist at Yaoqing Sports had some last-minute business, so I'm just here as a substitute. Please call me Amateur Journalist.

t_ch: 我的问题是…卢卡选手在赛前吃了什么？
t_en: My question is... What did Contestant Luka eat before the match?

t_ch: 谁会记这种事情…
t_en: Who would remember this sort of thing...

t_ch: 谁会记这种事情。我问你，你记得自己前天早饭吃了什么吗？
t_en: Who even remembers this? Let me ask you, do you remember what you ate for breakfast two days ago?

t_ch: 我记得啊，一碗小米粥加半个煎饼啊。
t_en: I do. A bowl of millet porridge and half a pancake.

t_ch: 他赛前会保持空腹…
t_en: He keeps an empty stomach before the match...

t_ch: 卢卡选手有一个习惯，他在赛前会尽可能保持空腹，这样在比赛过程中会更加清醒。
t_en: Luka has a habit of trying to keep an empty stomach before a match. This way, he can stay alert during the match.

t_ch: 确实，我吃饱了就犯困。
t_en: I agree. I get drowsy after eating.

t_ch: 吃的那可太多了…
t_en: Well, be ready for a long list then...

t_ch: 我想想啊，那可太多了。
t_en: Lemme think... Be ready for this long list then...

t_ch: 鸣藕糕、貘馍卷、苏打豆汁儿、琼实鸟串、陈婆豆腐、星芋扣肉、油爆飞海葵、糖球炒视肉、红油乱斩牛杂、蒜苗五花肉……
t_en: Songlotus cake, tuskpir wrap, mung bean soda, berrypheasant skewers, Granny Chen's Tofu, star taro braised pork, deep-fried flying sea anemone, sugarball-fried viscorpi, chili oil beef offal stew, garlic-infused pork...

t_ch: 您这是来报菜名了吗？演武仪典是大胃王比赛啊？
t_en: Are you reading off a menu? Is the Wardance a Competitive Eating Championship?

t_ch: 请记者提下一个问题。
t_en: Please ask the next question, journalist.

t_ch: 啊？还得提问啊，我想想…请问{NICKNAME}教练，您在赛前吃了什么？
t_en: Huh? I still have to ask another one? Lemme think... Coach {NICKNAME}, what did you eat before the match?

t_ch: 你到底是做什么的？
t_en: What do you exactly do as a job?

t_ch: 你怎么只关心吃的，你到底是做什么的？
t_en: Why do you only care about food? What exactly is your job?

t_ch: 呃…其实我本职工作是美食栏目主持人。
t_en: Uh... Actually, my main job is a food show host.

t_ch: 请注意你的问题…
t_en: Please watch your questions...

t_ch: 我是教练，不是厨师，请注意你的问题。
t_en: I'm a coach, not a chef. Please watch what you ask.

t_ch: 对不起，职业病犯了，总以为自己在主持美食栏目。
t_en: Sorry, it's just a professional habit. I keep thinking that I'm still hosting a food show.

t_ch: （沉默）
t_en: (Remains silent)

t_ch: 体育采访真的不适合我，我还是安心主持美食栏目吧，
t_en: Sports interviews really don't suit me. I think I should stick with hosting food shows.

t_ch: 美食栏目主持人也不这样工作啊！喂——
t_en: Even food show hosts don't work like this! Hey...

t_ch: 好了，这次提问就到这里。
t_en: Okay, the questioning ends here.

t_ch: 现在请{NICKNAME}教练再选一个记者提问。
t_en: Coach {NICKNAME}, please choose another journalist.

t_ch: 前排左手边第一位。
t_en: Front row, first from the left.

t_ch: 前排左手边第二位。
t_en: Front row, second from the left.

t_ch: 前排左手边第三位。
t_en: Front row, third from the left.

t_ch: 前排左手边第四位。
t_en: Front row, fourth from the left.

t_ch: 感谢{NICKNAME}教练的精彩答复，今天的采访环节到此结束，请各位来场的媒体朋友有序离场。
t_en: Thank you, Coach {NICKNAME}, for your insightful answers. Today's Q&A ends here. Media personnel, please leave the area in an orderly and civil fashion.

t_ch: 例行的采访环节已经结束，接下来该去办正事了。
t_en: Now that the routine interview is over, it's time to get down to business.

t_ch: 感谢卢卡选手再次给我们带来一场精彩的比赛！
t_en: Thank you, Contestant Luka, for bringing us yet another exciting match!

t_ch: 由于卢卡选手已经获得参加「竞锋守擂」的机会，所以这是{NICKNAME}教练最后一次接受采访，请记者朋友们珍惜提问机会。
t_en: As Luka has already obtained the opportunity to participate in the "Ringmaster's Challenge," this will be the last time Coach {NICKNAME} will be accepting interviews. Please cherish this opportunity to ask questions.

t_ch: 接下来开始由{NICKNAME}教练选择记者提问。
t_en: Next, Coach {NICKNAME} will be selecting journalists for questions.

t_ch: 前排左手边第一位。
t_en: Front row, first from the left.

t_ch: 请这位记者朋友提问。
t_en: Please ask your questions.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}教练你好，我是来自星际和平娱乐的实习记者卡美丽。你已经听过很多遍了——祝贺您和卢卡选手挺进守擂赛。
t_en: Hello, Coach {NICKNAME}, I am Carmella, and I am a journalist intern for Interastral Peace Entertainment. I'm sure you have heard this many times already, but congratulations to you and Luka on making the defending match!

t_ch: 谢谢。
t_en: Thank you.

t_ch: （点头表示感谢）
t_en: (Nods in appreciation)

t_ch: 本场雅利洛-Ⅵ内战比赛打得异常艰难，双方选手可谓是旗鼓相当，直到最后一刻才分出胜负。{NICKNAME}教练能评价下史瓦罗选手么？
t_en: The battle between Jarilo-VI's best was exceptionally tough, with both contestants evenly matched, and the outcome was not decided till the end. Coach {NICKNAME}, how would you evaluate Svarog's performance?

t_ch: 他是值得尊敬的对手。
t_en: He's an opponent that deserves our respect.

t_ch: 卢卡和史瓦罗在贝洛伯格时本就是宿命的对手，彼此之间知根知底。
t_en: Luka and Svarog have been destined rivals in Belobog, and they have complete understanding of each other.

t_ch: 尽管史瓦罗选手给我们晋级之路造成了很大的麻烦，但不得不承认他实力强大，是值得尊敬的对手。
t_en: Even though Svarog gave us a hard time on our path to advancement, we have to admit that he is formidable in strength and is a respectable opponent.

t_ch: 为冬城内战感到遗憾…
t_en: I feel regret for this match between kinsmen...

t_ch: 我们都是代表贝洛伯格参赛的，如果可以的话，我希望我们不用在守擂赛之前遭遇，但是现实就是如此遗憾，只有其中一方能够挑战守擂者。
t_en: We are all representing Belobog. If possible, we wish not to encounter each other before the final match. But the fact is as it is. Regrettably, there can only be one to challenge the ringmaster.

t_ch: 我们击败了史瓦罗选手，会带着他的遗憾一起，继续前进的。
t_en: We defeated Contestant Svarog and we will carry his regrets as we continue forward.

t_ch: 谁会不喜欢大机器人呢？
t_en: Who doesn't like big robots?

t_ch: 说实话，刚到雅利洛-Ⅵ时，我就意识到了，大机器人就是帅啊！
t_en: Honestly, when I first arrived in Jarilo-VI, I realized that big robots are really cool!

t_ch: 现在我也是这样的评价！
t_en: That's my evaluation now too!

t_ch: 原来如此——感谢你的回答！
t_en: I see... Thank you for your answer!

t_ch: 下一个问题。听说您是第一次担当教练，没想到已经取得了如此辉煌的战绩。请问您今后有打算担任全职教练吗？
t_en: Next question. I hear that this is your first time being a coach. It's extraordinary that you've achieved such stellar results. Do you intend to convert into a full-time coach in future?

t_ch: 我当然乐意啊，不过…
t_en: I'd be delighted, but...

t_ch: 我也没想到自己有当教练的天赋，如果有选手信任我，我当然乐意帮助。
t_en: I also never thought I'd have the talent for coaching. If a fighter trusts me, I'd of course be more than willing to assist.

t_ch: 只不过我相信，比我更专业的教练随处可见，最重要的还是自己的实力！
t_en: But I believe that there are many coaches who are more professional than me out there. What's crucial is your own ability!

t_ch: 不好意思，我只是个无名客。
t_en: Sorry, I'm just a Nameless.

t_ch: 当教练只是我一时兴起，全职工作并不适合我，我还是更喜欢做无名客。
t_en: I just became a coach on a whim. A full-time job isn't suited for me, and I much prefer being a Nameless still.

t_ch: 我的教学方法并不适合每个人…
t_en: My teaching methods aren't suited to everyone...

t_ch: 我的教学方法并不适合每个人。换句话说，卢卡与我相性比较好，也许换作其他人，我们就早早出局了。
t_en: My coaching methodology isn't for everyone. In other words, Luka and I are quite compatible. We might've been knocked out of the tournament if I had been with anyone else.

t_ch: 好了，这次提问就到这里了。
t_en: All right. That's it for your questions.

t_ch: 前排左手边第二位。
t_en: Front row, second from the left.

t_ch: 请这位记者朋友提问。
t_en: Please ask your questions.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}教练好啊，我是仙舟那啥啥网站特邀记者桂乃芬。我完全不知道你和卢卡选手真能闯进守擂赛啊！出乎我的意料！
t_en: Hello, Coach {NICKNAME}. I'm Guinaifen, special correspondent from that Xianzhou website, you know? I totally didn't know that you and Luka would really advance to the final stage! I'm completely floored!

t_ch: 谢谢，这下你知道了吧？
t_en: Thank you. Now you know, don't you?

t_ch: 知道啦知道啦，看把你得意的。
t_en: Yes yes, I got it. Look at you all smug.

t_ch: 你对我们这么没信心？
t_en: Did you have that little faith in us?

t_ch: 赖我赖我，是我看走眼了。
t_en: That's my bad. I misjudged you guys.

t_ch: 其实我关注到近期网络上的一些言论说两位选手都来自贝洛伯格，并且关系不错，有没有可能是史瓦罗选手故意放水…{NICKNAME}教练对此有何解释？
t_en: Reading recent comments on the net, they're saying that both contestants are from Belobog, and enjoy a cordial relationship. Is there a possibility that Svarog may have pulled his punches...? What does Coach {NICKNAME} have to say?

t_ch: 要问就问史瓦罗吧…
t_en: Just go ask Svarog...

t_ch: 史瓦罗选手是瞒着我们报名的，赛前我都不知道对手是他……
t_en: Svarog registered for this tournament behind our backs. We didn't even know he was our next opponent...

t_ch: 你这个问题还是问史瓦罗吧，不过我相信他尊重比赛，不会放水的。
t_en: You're better off asking Svarog instead, but I trust he did honor the tournament and gave it his all.

t_ch: 网友当裁判组是摆设…
t_en: Do the netizens think that the judges' panel is window dressing, or what?

t_ch: 网友当裁判组是摆设？要是史瓦罗选手真的放水，裁判组能看不出来？
t_en: They think the judges are window dressing? If Svarog were really trying to throw the match, you think the judges wouldn't notice?

t_ch: 网友们只是喜欢热门话题罢了，不会在意真相是什么。
t_en: Netizens just like to engage in rumor-mongering. They don't care about facts.

t_ch: 我没什么要解释的…
t_en: No explanation is required...

t_ch: 我没什么要解释的，清者自清，卢卡选手的实力有目共睹。
t_en: There's nothing to explain. Our consciences are clear as day, and Luka's prowess is evident to all.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}的教练的回答越来越有水平了！
t_en: Coach {NICKNAME}'s replies are getting more eloquent!

t_ch: 自从卢卡选手获得守擂赛资格后，网络上一些自称仙舟传统武术爱好者声称，卢卡选手不是仙舟人，按照传统根本没有资格参加守擂赛。
t_en: Ever since Luka attained the right to challenge the ringmaster, some self-proclaimed traditional Xianzhou martial artist hobbyists on the internet have claimed that Luka isn't from the Xianzhou, and according to tradition, shouldn't be allowed to participate in the final defending match.

t_ch: 我也不是本地人，觉得这些声音太刺耳了。请问{NICKNAME}教练有什么想说的吗？
t_en: I'm not local either, and I think these opinions are quite distasteful. Does coach {NICKNAME} have anything to say?

t_ch: 一群手下败将罢了。
t_en: They're just a bunch of sore losers.

t_ch: 不过是卢卡选手的手下败将，心有不甘却胆小如鼠，有本事在网上发发脾气，没有本事上擂台耍耍把式。
t_en: They are nothing more than fighters who lost to Luka — sore about their defeat but too cowardly to admit it. All they can do is vent their frustrations online since they lack the caliber in the ring.

t_ch: 其实卢卡也是仙舟传统武术…
t_en: Luka also practices traditional Xianzhou martial arts...

t_ch: 其实卢卡选手用的是仙舟传统拳法，没有想到吧？
t_en: Actually, Luka is using traditional Xianzhou martial arts. Bet you're surprised?

t_ch: 去找组委会评理啊！
t_en: Go complain to the committee then!

t_ch: 规则是组委会制定的，有本事去找组委会评理啊。
t_en: It was the committee that drew up the regulations. Go complain to the committee if they're up to it.

t_ch: 就是就是，还是{NICKNAME}说话有水平。
t_en: That's right. Coach {NICKNAME}'s response is eloquent as usual.

t_ch: 好了，这次提问就到这里了。
t_en: All right. That's it for your questions.

t_ch: 前排左手边第三位。
t_en: Front row, third from the left.

t_ch: 请这位记者朋友提问。
t_en: Please ask your questions.

t_ch: 你知道我是谁，我就不介绍了哦。
t_en: You know who I am, so I'll skip the introduction.

t_ch: 我记得，卢卡之前是打不过史瓦罗大佬的，{NICKNAME}，他到底是为什么打赢的？
t_en: I remember that Luka couldn't defeat Boss Svarog before. So, {NICKNAME}, how exactly did he do it?

t_ch: 史瓦罗选手老了…
t_en: Svarog is getting old...

t_ch: 史瓦罗大佬固然很强，但他在雅利洛-Ⅵ星核之灾出现前就存在了，岁数有点太大了。反观卢卡选手还是个小伙子，正值当打之年。
t_en: Boss Svarog is very strong, but he's an antique who existed before the Jarilo-VI's Stellaron Disaster even appeared. Conversely, Luka's still young and vibrant, a fighter in his prime.

t_ch: 俗话说「拳怕少壮」，所以不是卢卡变强了，而是史瓦罗大佬变弱了。
t_en: As the saying goes: The fist fears the strength of youth. It's not that Luka got stronger, but Svarog became weaker.

t_ch: 这就是成长的力量。
t_en: This is the power of growth.

t_ch: 卢卡选手一路走来遇到不少强敌，所谓「打不倒你的会使你更强大」，在一系列的比赛历练之后，他的肉体和精神都得到了强化。
t_en: Luka has encountered many powerful opponents along the way. "What doesn't kill you makes you stronger" — after a series of fights, his physique and spirit have been strengthened.

t_ch: 现在的卢卡与过去的卢卡已经不一样了，这就是成长的力量。
t_en: Luka as he is now is different from that Luka of past. This is the power of growth.

t_ch: 这就是教练的作用。
t_en: That's what a coach is for.

t_ch: 竞技体育与他们俩之前的交手都不太一样。选手自身实力固然重要，合适的战术也会起到举足轻重的作用。
t_en: Competitive fighting is quite different from the fights they got into against each other before. While the opponent's strength is an important consideration, the right tactics are also crucial in turning the tide.

t_ch: 史瓦罗选手的力量、技巧、速度都不比卢卡选手差，只是缺个好教练。
t_en: Svarog's strength, skill, and speed are not inferior to Luka's. He's only missing a good coach.

t_ch: 原来如此，虎克根本没有听懂。
t_en: I see. Hook didn't understand a word you said.

t_ch: 我听说下一场卢卡要打彦卿，据说是比虎克还要天才的剑士，{NICKNAME}怎么办？
t_en: I hear that Luka has to fight Yanqing in his next match. They say he's an even more talented swordmaster than Hook! What are you going to do, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 卢卡有没有换个功率加倍的机械臂呢？或者有没有做过「空手夺白刃」的训练呢？你的战术准备好了吗？
t_en: Did Luka change to a more powerful robot arm? Or does he have prior training to catch blades with his bare hands? Have you prepared your battle tactics?

t_ch: 虎克，不许透露我的笔记！
t_en: Hey, Hook, don't leak my notes like that!

t_ch: 虎克，你是不是偷看了我的笔记？怎么把我的战术全都说出来了，彦卿要是全知道了，我岂不是白准备了。
t_en: Did you sneak a peek at my notes, Hook? How could you reveal all my tactics? If Yanqing knows everything, my preparation will go up in smoke!

t_ch: 接下来的比赛只与实力有关。
t_en: The upcoming matches will only be about their skill.

t_ch: 守擂赛可不是小打小闹，说实话，战术能起的作用有限。卢卡能走多远，完全得看他个人实力了。
t_en: There's no fooling around in the final defending match. To be honest, there's a limit to how effective tactics can be. How far Luka can go completely depends on his own individual skill.

t_ch: 我的战术用完了…
t_en: I've already used up all my tactics...

t_ch: 前面的比赛早就把我准备的战术用完了，接下来听天由命吧。
t_en: I've already exhausted all my tactics in the previous matches. Now I can only leave it up to fate.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，我完全明白了。
t_en: Yep, I totally understand.

t_ch: 好了，这次提问就到这里了。
t_en: All right. That's it for your questions.

t_ch: 现在请{NICKNAME}教练再选一个记者提问。
t_en: Coach {NICKNAME}, please choose another journalist.

t_ch: 前排左手边第一位。
t_en: Front row, first from the left.

t_ch: 前排左手边第二位。
t_en: Front row, second from the left.

t_ch: 前排左手边第三位。
t_en: Front row, third from the left.

t_ch: 感谢{NICKNAME}教练的精彩答复，今天的采访环节到此结束，请各位来场的媒体朋友有序离场。
t_en: Thank you, Coach {NICKNAME}, for your insightful answers. Today's Q&A ends here. Media personnel, please leave the area in an orderly and civil fashion.

t_ch: 例行的采访环节已经结束，接下来该去办正事了。
t_en: Now that the routine interview is over, it's time to get down to business.

t_ch: 休赛期结束啦，卢卡选手，做好继续比赛的准备了吗？
t_en: The break is over. Contestant Luka, are you ready to keep competing?

t_ch: 早就等不及了，赶紧开始吧。
t_en: I can't wait. Let's get this started.

t_ch: 先别急，我们来一起看看下场擂台赛的对手信息。
t_en: Hold on. Let's look at the opponent information for the next Faceoff Match first.

t_ch: 一段时间后，贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆……
t_en: After a while, at the Belobog History and Culture Museum...

t_ch: 与此同时，竞锋舰甲板上。
t_en: At the same time, on the Skysplitter's deck...

t_ch: 有机生命，歼灭！歼灭！歼灭！
t_en: Organic life. Annihilate! Annihilate! Annihilate!

t_ch: 反有机方程的残存么，还是第一次见…罢了，倒也并非不可度化。
t_en: Remnants of the anti-organic equation, huh? This is the first time I've seen one... Never mind, it's not that it cannot be delivered to salvation.

t_ch: 坏劫星群，距演武仪典开始还有<unbreak>51</unbreak>个系统日。
t_en: Destroyed Star Cluster, <unbreak>51</unbreak> System Days before the start of the Wardance.

t_ch: 飞船拘禁区，距演武仪典开始还有3个系统日。
t_en: Spaceship's Restricted Zone, 3 System Days before the start of the Wardance.

t_ch: 护法僧毗梨、智众经师驮那，为一万三千人请愿，于星辰间圆寂。愿您勿遗忘他们的名字。
t_en: Dharmic Monk Virya and Revered Master Dhyana left this world on their journey across the stars to petition for thirteen thousand living beings. Please, do not forget their names.

t_ch: 皈依弟子善逝，断离恶业，为生民静默，于此地长眠。愿您能记得它最后的选择。
t_en: The converted disciple, Sugata, severed evil karma and kept silence for the sake of the people, and now rests here for all eternity. May you remember their final choice.

t_ch: 金人巷，杜氏茶庄。
t_en: Du's Teahouse, Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 杰帕德先生比我预想中还要强得多…不行，决不能输。
t_en: Mr. Gepard is much stronger than I expected... No, I can't lose.

t_ch: 整个贝洛伯格都在看着我，我不能输！
t_en: All of Belobog is watching me! I can't lose!

t_ch: 体力耗尽了，我恐怕撑不了太久了…
t_en: I'm out of stamina. I don't think I can hold out much longer...

t_ch: 不行，整个贝城的人都看着我呢，我不能输！
t_en: No, all of Belobog is watching me! I can't lose!

t_ch: 到此为止了。
t_en: That's enough.

t_ch: 每个人都在为卢卡选手而尖叫！
t_en: Everyone is screaming for Luka!

t_ch: 卢卡选手点燃了全场的热情！
t_en: Luka has set the arena ablaze with excitement!

t_ch: 卢卡选手化解了对手的攻势！
t_en: Luka efficiently neutralizes his opponent's attack!

t_ch: 卢卡选手！创造奇迹的男人！
t_en: Luka! The miracle man!

t_ch: 卢卡选手扭转了战局！太强了！
t_en: Luka has turned the tables! Legendary!

t_ch: 卢卡选手再度创造了奇迹！不愧是卢卡选手！
t_en: Luka pulls off another miracle with his bare hands! What a man!

t_ch: 真不愧是卢卡选手！
t_en: No doubt about it, Luka is the real deal!

t_ch: 永不言败的卢卡选手！继续战斗！
t_en: The indomitable Luka never gives up! The fight continues!

t_ch: 卢卡选手能再度逆转战局吗？
t_en: Can Luka turn the tides again?

t_ch: 卢卡选手被压制住了！这下他该怎么办！
t_en: Luka is on the defensive! What will he do?

t_ch: 对手加强了攻势！大概是急着下班收工吧！
t_en: The opponent has stepped up their offense! They are probably trying to end it quickly so they can get off work early!

t_ch: 对手再次袭来！卢卡选手，你要如何应对！
t_en: The opponent strikes again! How will Luka react?

t_ch: 对手来势汹汹！不要输啊卢卡选手！
t_en: The opponent is coming in hot! Hang in there, Luka!

t_ch: 对手不打算给卢卡选手任何机会！
t_en: The opponent is not leaving any openings for Luka!

t_ch: 卢卡，快用那招！他们猜不到的！
t_en: Luka, quickly use that move! They'll never see it coming!

t_ch: 卢卡，振作一点！快出拳！
t_en: Luka, get it together! Throw a punch!

t_ch: 卢卡，从那个角度出拳吧！
t_en: Luka, change your angle, and punch!

t_ch: 为了我，对对手使用铁拳吧！
t_en: Keep calm, and use your iron fist against your opponent! Do it for me!

t_ch: 稳住，用这招对付对手！
t_en: Use this move against your opponent!

t_ch: 不要怕，用这招一定能赢！
t_en: Never fear. You'll definitely win against your opponent with this move!

t_ch: 先给你们科普一下～在「演武仪典」的擂台上，观众的氛围可是很重要的哦！
t_en: Lemme remind you! On the Wardance stage, the atmosphere of the spectators is very important!

t_ch: 展现出你华丽的招式，让观众为你尖叫吧！
t_en: Show off your flashy moves and make the audience cheer for you!

t_ch: 兄弟姐妹们，该我们表演了！
t_en: Brothers and sisters, it's time for us to show off our stuff!

t_ch: 仙舟翾翔，云骑常胜！仙舟翾翔，云骑常胜！
t_en: Xianzhou soars, Cloud Knight roars! Xianzhou soars, Cloud Knight roars! Xianzhou soars, Cloud Knight roars!...

t_ch: 卢卡选手似乎难以构成有效的反击！该怎么办呢？卢卡选手！
t_en: Luka seems to be struggling to land a counter! What is Luka going to do?

t_ch: 卢卡选手化解了云骑军的攻势，逆转了战局！
t_en: Luka deflects the Cloud Knights' attack and turns the tide of the battle!

t_ch: 卢卡选手没能化解云骑军的攻势！再次被压制！
t_en: Luka isn't able to deflect the Cloud Knights' attack! He's pinned down once again!

t_ch: 兄弟姐妹们，快振作起来！小桂子还在看着呢！
t_en: Brothers and sisters, get it together! Lil Gui is watching!

t_ch: 这上来的…怎么是裂界造物？我这是…出现幻觉了？
t_en: This... Why is there a Fragmentum creation? Am I... hallucinating?

t_ch: 来吧，让我们制胜一击！兄弟姐妹们！不要给他第二次机会！
t_en: Come, let's deal the winning blow! Don't give him another chance!

t_ch: 「桂宝应援会」似乎做好了准备，要彻底终结比赛！
t_en: Looks like Lil Gui's Support Association is ready to end this match once and for all!

t_ch: 呃啊…怎么会……
t_en: Oh no... How can it be...

t_ch: 卢卡选手被击倒了！他是否还能重新站起来，创造奇迹呢？
t_en: Luka has been knocked down! Can he rise again and produce another miracle?

t_ch: 喂！{NICKNAME}教练，你可不能光看着啊！
t_en: Hey! Coach {NICKNAME}, don't just stand there staring!

t_ch: 那我…上去帮他打你们？
t_en: Then... should I go up there myself and defeat you guys for him?

t_ch: 哎呀！当然不是了！来，我来教你～
t_en: Ugh! Of course not! Come, let me show you how it's done!

t_ch: 卢卡！他们人多势众，但个体能力不如你，尝试各个击破他们！
t_en: Luka! They have the numbers, but their abilities are not on par with yours. So try and take them down one by one!

t_ch: 虽然不至于借用琥珀王的伟力，但我们还是不会留情的哦。
t_en: We may not call upon the Amber Lord's might, but that doesn't mean we'll go easy on you.

t_ch: 能击倒卢卡的员工！我会在绩效总评中给你打高分！
t_en: Those who can knock down Luka! I'll be sure to give you an excellent score in your performance evaluation!

t_ch: 上啊！兄弟们！为了绩效！
t_en: Let's roll, people! For our performance evaluation!

t_ch: 手下员工尽数倒下！托帕选手似乎准备亲自上场了！
t_en: Her subordinate forces have almost been whittled down! It looks like Topaz is preparing to take to the field herself!

t_ch: 没办法，有些事还是要亲力亲为才行。
t_en: Not like I have a choice. If you want something done properly, do it yourself.

t_ch: 可…可可利亚大人？
t_en: M... Madam Cocolia?

t_ch: 啊？你刚才叫我什么？
t_en: Huh? What did you just call me?

t_ch: 喔？卢卡选手怎么突然愣住了？
t_en: Oh? It appears Luka is dazed all of a sudden.

t_ch: 不…没什么…来吧！托帕小姐！
t_en: No... It's nothing... Come at me, Miss Topaz!

t_ch: （糟糕，我的幻觉似乎更严重了。）
t_en: (Oh no, my hallucinations are getting worse.)

t_ch: 看来是时候稍微认真一点了。
t_en: Looks like it's time to get serious.

t_ch: 你打得很漂亮，卢卡先生。
t_en: You fight well, Mr. Luka.

t_ch: 过家家的游戏，该结束了！
t_en: But it's time to end this game of playing house!

t_ch: 好强…她之前果然没有用全力……
t_en: So strong... She wasn't even using her full power just now...

t_ch: 卢卡选手完全被托帕选手压制住了！这束手无策的局面，是否还能挽回！
t_en: Luka has been completely cornered by Topaz! Will he be able to turn the tide on his precarious predicament?

t_ch: 动起来啊！卢卡！磐岩镇的拳王可不能在这里倒下！
t_en: Keep moving, Luka! The boxing champ of Boulder Town can't fall here!

t_ch: 抱歉，但看来属于你的比赛，就要到此结束了。
t_en: Apologies, but it looks like this match that should've been yours will end here.

t_ch: 两位选手一动不动，在擂台两边对峙！
t_en: Our two contestants haven't moved a muscle, as they face each other on opposite sides of the ring!

t_ch: 面对波提欧选手的枪口，卢卡选手要做何选择！
t_en: Just what will Luka do in the face of Boothill's muzzle?

t_ch: 比赛双方还没有动作！全场观众的神经都快要绷断了！
t_en: No action yet from either side! All the spectators are on the edge of their seats, their nerves on the brink of snapping!

t_ch: 快逃吧卢卡，别过去，你会死的。
t_en: Run away, now, Luka. Don't go over there, you'll die!

t_ch: 我…不…我不能……
t_en: No... I... I can't...

t_ch: 哦？你连走上前来的勇气都没有吗？
t_en: Oh? Don't even have the courage to step closer...

t_ch: 你不该与他对阵的，卢卡！他会杀了你！
t_en: You shouldn't be fighting him, Luka! He'll kill you!

t_ch: 一个真正的拳手，绝不会屈服于恐惧。
t_en: A true boxer doesn't succumb to fear!

t_ch: 我不能死在这里，我还不想死…我还不想死！
t_en: I can't die here. I don't want to die... I don't want to die yet!

t_ch: 卢卡！快清醒一点！
t_en: Luka! Snap out of it!

t_ch: 我再说最后一遍——
t_en: I'll say this one last time...

t_ch: 要么转身离开，要么过来揍我。
t_en: Either turn around and leave, or get over here and hit me.

t_ch: 卢卡！子弹不长眼啊！难道你真的想死吗！
t_en: Luka! Bullets don't have eyes! Do you really want to die?

t_ch: 现在只要转身离开，就什么都不会发生，快逃吧！
t_en: Just turn around and leave! Nothing will happen! Run!

t_ch: 不，我不能退缩……
t_en: No, I won't back down...

t_ch: 小子，这样犹豫不决吗？我他呜呜伯的对你很失望。
t_en: What's with the indecisiveness, kid? Diggity Wubbaboo, I'm so disappointed in you.

t_ch: 卢卡！振作一点！
t_en: Luka! Get it together!

t_ch: 那么，考验正式开始了。
t_en: Well, let the trial begin.

t_ch: 卢卡选手，请做好准备吧。
t_en: Luka, prepare yourself.

t_ch: 卢卡选手，邀你与我共舞一曲——
t_en: Mr. Luka, you're invited to perform...

t_ch: ——纯美的轮舞！
t_en: ...the rondo of Beauty with me!

t_ch: 卢卡选手究竟能否通过银枝的考验呢！好紧张啊！
t_en: Can Luka pass Argenti's trial? I'm so nervous!

t_ch: 银枝先生，这几招怎么样？
t_en: Mr. Argenti, what do you think of these moves?

t_ch: 我会给你最后一次机会，请好好把握。
t_en: I'm giving you one last chance. Please do your best.

t_ch: 这真是一场非常精彩的比赛。
t_en: What an exciting match.

t_ch: 那么接下来……
t_en: Next...

t_ch: 卢卡选手，邀你与我共谱一曲——
t_en: Mr. Luka, you're invited to compose...

t_ch: ——纯美的颂歌！
t_en: ...an anthem of Beauty with me!

t_ch: 银枝先生，这一拳这称得上「纯美」吗？
t_en: Mr. Argenti, does this punch count as "the Beauty?"

t_ch: 请认真一点吧！卢卡选手！
t_en: Please take this seriously, Luka!

t_ch: 冷静点，卢卡，将你最勇敢的一面展现给银枝吧！
t_en: Keep your cool, Luka. Let Argenti see your bravest side!

t_ch: 打得很好，卢卡选手。
t_en: That's amazing, Luka.

t_ch: 再次提示——我不会放水。请你小心。
t_en: Again, I'd like to remind you that I won't make this easy for you. Please be careful.

t_ch: 嗯，感谢你愿意出全力！史瓦罗大佬！
t_en: Got it. Thanks for not holding back, Svarog!

t_ch: 史瓦罗！不要输哦！
t_en: Come on Svarog, don't lose!

t_ch: 卢卡哥哥！也不要输哦！
t_en: Come on Luka, don't lose!

t_ch: 你到底支持哪一边啦！
t_en: Whose side are you on?

t_ch: 计算——这不是你当前的最佳水平，请认真与我战斗。
t_en: According to my calculations, this is not the best you can do. Please treat this battle seriously.

t_ch: 喔！卢卡哥哥要赢了！史瓦罗加油呀！
t_en: Oh! Luka's about to win! Svarog, don't give up!

t_ch: …但是卢卡哥哥也不可以输哦！
t_en: ...But I want you to win too, Luka!

t_ch: 真是个不讲理的孩子。
t_en: I can't handle that girl.

t_ch: 哇哦～史瓦罗选手似乎准备全力一击了！
t_en: Woah! Looks like Svarog is gearing up for an all-out attack!

t_ch: 卢卡哥哥！不要输给史瓦罗啊！
t_en: Luka, don't lose to Svarog!

t_ch: 两名战士已经就位！
t_en: Both fighters are ready!

t_ch: 这场万众瞩目的决战！
t_en: The final showdown!

t_ch: 正式开始！
t_en: Begins now!

t_ch: 彦卿大人的飞剑环绕着竞锋舰的擂台……
t_en: Yanqing's flying swords are swirling around the ring...

t_ch: 卢卡选手究竟该如何应对呢？
t_en: How will Luka deal with them?

t_ch: 卢卡选手击落了彦卿大人的飞剑！
t_en: Luka has knocked down Yanqing's swords!

t_ch: 这是个非常好的开始！
t_en: He's off to a flying start!

t_ch: 看来卢卡选手真是位了不得的战士！
t_en: Luka's truly a magnificent warrior!

t_ch: 彦卿大人开始动真格的了！
t_en: Yanqing's starting taking this more seriously!

t_ch: 彦卿大人即将使出他最为得意的杀招！
t_en: Yanqing's about to unleash his signature move!

t_ch: 卢卡选手！你是否能接下这一剑！
t_en: Luka! Will you be able to take this?

t_ch: 你的飞剑真是坚硬，我这铁臂已经完全失灵了……
t_en: Your swords are so tough. My iron arm has gone kaput...

t_ch: 既然如此，我也将弃剑不用。就让我们用双拳决定最后的胜者吧。
t_en: In this case, I'll stop using my swords. Let's decide who the winner is with our bare fists.

t_ch: 上次侥幸被你偷袭成功，这次我要好好收拾你。
t_en: You were lucky that you got me in that ambush last time. I'm gonna fix you up real good this time.

t_ch: 卢卡退赛！
t_en: Luka, just withdraw!

t_ch: 明明是他在背后搞小动作，为什么全场都在为他加油呐喊？
t_en: He's the one who's playing dirty, but why is the entire stadium cheering for him?

t_ch: 感觉身体好重，胃在抽搐，拳头也挥不动了，我需要休息。
t_en: My body feels so heavy... My stomach hurts... And I can't swing my fists anymore... I need to rest.

t_ch: 所有人都在讨厌我，我是不是真的应该退赛？好累，我想回家。
t_en: Everyone hates me. Should I just quit? I'm so tired. I wanna go home.

t_ch: 卢卡，保持大脑清晰。
t_en: Luka, keep your head straight.

t_ch: 想想你来这里的目的，不要被他们影响。
t_en: Think about why you're here. Don't let them affect you.

t_ch: 混小子，还有场外支援是吧，你可真行！
t_en: You rascal... You've got helpers outside the ring now, do you?

t_ch: 黑幕！黑幕！黑幕！
t_en: Rigged! Rigged! Rigged!

t_ch: 铄金滚蛋
t_en: Shuojin, get lost!

t_ch: 满嘴谎言
t_en: Big fat liar.

t_ch: 令人作呕
t_en: Disgusting.

t_ch: 滚回公司
t_en: Get your arse back to the IPC.

t_ch: 卢卡居然还不退赛，脸皮真厚啊。
t_en: I can't believe Luka's still continuing with the matches, what a shameless person.

t_ch: 赶紧退赛吧，别来玷污演武仪典！
t_en: Give up now! Don't taint the Wardance ceremony!

t_ch: 卢卡用盘外招，真是令人恶心。
t_en: Luka using underhanded tactics is truly disgusting.

t_ch: 铄金好好打，替我揍扁卢卡！
t_en: Fight well, Shuojin! Give Luka a good beating!

t_ch: 我从未见过像卢卡这样的无耻之人。
t_en: I have never seen someone as audacious as Luka.

t_ch: 卢卡，滚回你老家，罗浮不欢迎你。
t_en: Go home, Luka. You're not welcome on the Luofu.

t_ch: 还赖着不走
t_en: What are you even doing here?

t_ch: 呸呸呸
t_en: I spit on you.

t_ch: 滚蛋
t_en: Get out of here.

t_ch: 卢卡退赛
t_en: Luka needs to quit.

t_ch: 下手真黑
t_en: No morals.

t_ch: 公司走狗
t_en: IPC dog.

t_ch: 铄金还想骗我们，赶紧滚蛋！
t_en: Shuojin is still trying to fool us, scram!

t_ch: 这家伙满嘴谎言，卢卡差点被他害了。
t_en: This guy's full of lies, Luka almost became his victim.

t_ch: 呸呸！真是令人作呕，亏我以前还信了铄金的鬼话。
t_en: Ugh! It's truly nauseating. I can't believe I used to fall for Shuojin's lies.

t_ch: 你这条公司的走狗，赶紧滚回公司。
t_en: Go back to Pier Point, IPC lackey.

t_ch: 一段时间后，「星天演武仪典」新闻发布会现场……
t_en: Some time later, at the venue of the Luminary Wardance press conference...

t_ch: 一段时间后，竞锋舰报刊亭附近……
t_en: Some time later, near the Skysplitter newsstand...

t_ch: 我的妈呀！这不是大名鼎鼎的{NICKNAME}吗！好巧啊，没想到会在这里遇见你！
t_en: Holy moly! Isn't this the world-famous {NICKNAME}? What a coincidence! I can't believe I'm meeting you here!

t_ch: 自我介绍一下，我叫召夏，是地衡司的勤务。没想到我这样的小人物也有资格认识你啊！
t_en: Allow me to introduce myself, I'm Zhaoxia, an orderly at the Realm-Keeping Commission. I can't believe a small fry like me would have the honor of meeting you!

t_ch: 要签名吗？
t_en: You want an autograph?

t_ch: 帝弓在上！传说中的{NICKNAME}竟然要给我签名？
t_en: By the Arbiter! The legendary {NICKNAME} is willing to give me an autograph?

t_ch: 不要迷恋哥，哥只是个传说。
t_en: Don't simp over me. I'm just a legend.

t_ch: 求你了，别玩这比我都老的梗好吗？好的。
t_en: I'm begging you, please don't use that new-generation lingo with me ok? Okay.

t_ch: 你怎么这么亢奋？
t_en: Why are you so hyped?

t_ch: 当然是因为见到偶像，所以心情比较激动了啊！
t_en: It's because I'm meeting my idol! Of course I'm hyped!

t_ch: 对不起我走错房间了。
t_en: Sorry, I must have entered the wrong room.

t_ch: 别走！求你别走！
t_en: Don't go! I'm begging you!

t_ch: 你累吗？要不要找个椅子坐一会？你渴了吗？红茶绿茶花草茶这里都有。
t_en: Are you tired? Do you need a chair to sit on? You thirsty? I have red tea, green tea, and floral tea!

t_ch: 你饿了吗？啊！对了，你稍等我一会儿！我去给你煮一碗牛肉面！
t_en: Are you hungry? Ah! Wait here! I'll make you a bowl of beef noodles!

t_ch: 召夏转身就要离开，似乎准备找地方给你煮一碗牛肉面。
t_en: Zhaoxia turns to leave, seemingly preparing to find a place to make you a bowl of beef noodles.

t_ch: 我不吃牛肉。
t_en: I don't eat beef.

t_ch: 不用麻烦了，我不饿。
t_en: Don't trouble yourself, I'm not hungry.

t_ch: 你的精神状态真的还正常吗？
t_en: Are you all right up there?

t_ch: 看到你没有吃面的兴致，召夏似乎突然恢复了理智，深深叹了一口气。
t_en: Seeing that you're not keen on the noodles, Zhaoxia seems to regain his senses and takes a deep breath.

t_ch: 唉…抱歉，我是不是吓到你了？
t_en: Ah... Sorry, did I scare you?

t_ch: 是有点吓人。
t_en: Kinda.

t_ch: 这不算什么。
t_en: It's nothing.

t_ch: 我的粉丝一般都很狂热。
t_en: My fans are always this fanatical anyways.

t_ch: 对不起，其实我算不上你的粉丝…我是说，我的确很欣赏你啦！但还没到粉丝那个地步……
t_en: Sorry, I can't exactly say I'm your fan... What I'm saying is I really admire you! But not to the point where I'm a fan...

t_ch: 只是，你是这场「庙会」的第二个客人…保不齐也就是最后一个了，我实在是怕你跑了。
t_en: But you're the second guest at this temple fair... And you could very well be the last one. I'm actually afraid you might run away.

t_ch: 第二个？第一个是谁？
t_en: Second one? Who's the first?

t_ch: 是一位年轻的女孩子。没想到这个时代，还有年轻人对我们的「庙会」感兴趣！而且她还玩得特别开心，实在是令我感动！
t_en: The first one was a young lady. Who knew that there are still youngsters interested in our temple fair in this era! She looked like she had fun, I was very touched!

t_ch: 地衡司分拨给「庙会」的预算越来越少，对年轻人越来越缺乏吸引力……
t_en: The budget allocated for temple fairs by the Realm-Keeping Commission is dwindling, making it less appealing to youngsters year after year...

t_ch: 今年又撞上演武仪典的档期，更没人来参加什么「庙会」了，说实话，有两个客人已经远超预期了！
t_en: The temple fair even coincides with the Wardance this year, so even fewer people attended. Honestly, having two guests already exceeds my expectations!

t_ch: 「庙会」？那庙呢？
t_en: Temple fair? Where's the temple?

t_ch: 「庙会」这东西其实是古国时代就有的风俗。过节的时候大家聚集在寺庙附近，进行酬神的表演。后来就演变成了大家一起过节玩乐的集市。
t_en: The temple fair is actually a tradition from the Primeval Imperium Era. During festivals, everyone will gather near temples to perform rituals for the gods. Later, it evolved into a market where people would gather to play games together.

t_ch: 以前「寺庙」是大家生活中特别重要的一部分，所以才有「庙会」。
t_en: Back then, temples were a huge part of people's lives, and that's why there were temple fairs.

t_ch: 但现在，帝弓司命又不需要咱凡人建庙祭祀，自然也就没有什么「寺庙」了。
t_en: But now the Reignbow Arbiter no longer requires us mortals to build temples and offer sacrifices, and the need for temples naturally went away.

t_ch: 但请你放心！只要你参加了咱们的「庙会游戏」，一定会发自内心地大喊一句——「妙啊！」
t_en: But don't you worry! As long as you join our temple fair games, your heart will surely be shouting: Fair-bulous!

t_ch: 「罗浮庙会，庙在哪里？妙在你心里！」
t_en: "How was the Luofu Temple Fair? It was fair-bulous!"

t_ch: （唯有沉默）
t_en: (Remain Silent)

t_ch: 好吧，另一位客人听完也沉默了。你们俩的沉默真的有点伤人……
t_en: Fine, the other guest also didn't say anything when I said that. The silence from both of you is quite hurtful...

t_ch: 太冷了，我尬住了。
t_en: Wow. That was really cringe.

t_ch: 呃…另一位客人听完就沉默了…这广告词那么烂吗？我构思了一星期呢！
t_en: Hmph... The other guest also didn't say anything when I said that... Is this tagline so terrible? I thought about it for a week!

t_ch: 你这广告词真不错。
t_en: This tagline's not bad.

t_ch: 真的吗？你喜欢就好！另一位客人听完就沉默了，搞得我差点就失去自信了！
t_en: Really? As long as you like it! The other guest didn't say anything. I almost lost all confidence!

t_ch: 但我还是不知道庙会要干啥。
t_en: But I don't know what the temple fair is about.

t_ch: 其实就是以前那些传统庙会游戏的交感幻戏升级版～以前你要亲自到线下去体验的那些经典仙舟游戏，现在可以在庙会上一站式体验了！是不是很有趣？
t_en: It's actually an improved Empathic Immersia of those traditional temple fair games you used to know! The traditional Xianzhou games you had to be there personally to experience are now all available at this temple fair! Isn't it amazing?

t_ch: 以前我就不爱玩。
t_en: I never enjoyed those before.

t_ch: 别这么说嘛！以前是以前，现在是现在！
t_en: Don't say that! The past is the past. You're living in the now!

t_ch: 听起来不错，让我试试吧。
t_en: Sounds decent. Let me try.

t_ch: 太好了！我就知道你会感兴趣的！
t_en: Awesome! I knew you would be interested!

t_ch: 如果你准备参加庙会，就跟我说一声吧。我帮你链接体验设备～
t_en: If you're planning to attend the temple fair, just let me know. I'll help you link up the experience device.

t_ch: 我要逛庙会。
t_en: I'm gonna take a tour of the temple fair.

t_ch: 还有别的事。
t_en: Nah, I've got better stuff to do.

t_ch: 好嘞！忙完了随时来找我！反正我这也没别的客人……
t_en: Right you are! Feel free to come to me once you're done! It's not like I have any other guests anyway...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，要不要再来逛逛庙会呀？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, do you want to take a tour of the temple fair?

t_ch: 我要逛庙会。
t_en: I'm gonna take a tour of the temple fair.

t_ch: 还有别的事。
t_en: Nah, I've got better stuff to do.

t_ch: 好嘞！忙完了随时来找我！反正我这也没别的客人……
t_en: Right you are! Feel free to come to me once you're done! It's not like I have any other guests anyway...

t_ch: 哇！这个魔方真的好好玩耶～
t_en: Wow! This magic cube is really fun!

t_ch: 真的耶！枘凿六合真是世界上最好玩的游戏！
t_en: You're right! Hexanexus truly is the most enjoyable game in the world!

t_ch: 召夏，你的庙会闹鬼了！
t_en: Zhaoxia, your temple fair is haunted!

t_ch: 这是什么恐怖游戏吗？
t_en: Is this some horror game?

t_ch: 捉鬼小队！捉鬼小队集结！
t_en: Ghost-hunting squad! Assemble!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}玩家，请别激动。这些只是人工智能陪玩罢了。
t_en: Simmer down, player {NICKNAME}. These are just AI NPCs to make up the numbers.

t_ch: 大毫？你怎么在这里？
t_en: Dahao? What're you doing here?

t_ch: 因为我将自己的形象授权给了「罗浮庙会筹备组」，所以他们可以让虚拟大毫担任庙会引导员。
t_en: I've transferred my image rights to the Luofu Temple Fair Preparatory Group, so they can use my virtual emulation as a temple fair guide.

t_ch: 大毫？你怎么死了？
t_en: Dahao? How'd you die?

t_ch: 别担心，我还没死。我只是将自己的形象授权给了「罗浮庙会筹备组」，所以他们可以让虚拟大毫担任庙会引导员。
t_en: Don't worry. I'm not dead. I've just transferred my image rights to the Luofu Temple Fair Preparatory Group so they can use my virtual emulation as a temple fair guide.

t_ch: 你说话实在不像真的大毫。
t_en: You don't sound like the real Dahao at all.

t_ch: 预算削减太严重了，我们雇不起专职文案了。虚拟陪玩的对话台本，都是用点小恩小惠，雇幽囚狱里一个叫阿合马的步离人写的。
t_en: The budget was severely slashed, and we couldn't hire a professional writer. We called in a lot of favors to hire a borisin called Arma from the Shackling Prison to write the dialogue script for the virtual NPCs.

t_ch: 「阿合马铁窗集团竭诚为您服务！零食饮料，日化百货，未语先笑，代客煎药！您的需要就是阿合马铁窗集团奋斗的目标！」
t_en: "The Arma Correctional Group is sincerely at your service! Snacks and drinks, daily necessities, service with a smile, medicine for our guests! We aim for your every need at Arma Correctional Group!"

t_ch: 抱歉，讲到关键词，触发小连招了。
t_en: Sorry, saying keywords triggers my tagline.
